mnemonics: C.I.A. (for Structure Initialization): C for Compiler, I for Initializer, A for Assignment. Remember: The Compiler needs an Initializer list for Assignment.
B.R.A.C.E. (Brackets): Braces Required At Creation/Construction for Elements.
keyPoints: Structure initialization assigns initial values to its members at the point of declaration.
The most common method is using an initializer list enclosed in curly braces `{}`.
Members are assigned values from the initializer list in the order they are declared in the structure definition.
commonMistakes: Providing more initializers than there are members in the structure, which causes a compiler error.
Mismatching the order of initializers with the order of member declarations, leading to incorrect value assignment.
Forgetting that uninitialized members may contain garbage values if not explicitly set.
